{"description":"## Meta
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Platform | Flutter (iOS + Android) |
| Privacy model | On-device only — no network, no cloud |

---

## Problem statement
Users need to remember context-specific tasks when they arrive at a physical location (e.g. grocery items at a store, errands at a hardware store). Existing todo apps are not location-aware. Existing location-aware apps require cloud accounts and sacrifice privacy.

## Goal
A native-feeling, fully private mobile app that lets users attach todo lists to geographic locations and receive a local notification when they enter that location's boundary.

---

## Architecture

### Layer stack (top → bottom, strict dependency direction)
```
UI (Flutter widgets)
  └── ViewModel (screen state, no business logic)
        └── Domain (use cases, pure Dart, zero dependencies)
              └── Repository (abstracts all data sources)
                    ├── SQLite via drift (todos, lists, geofences)
                    ├── OS Geofence API (Core Location / GeofencingClient)
                    └── OS Notification API (UNUserNotification / NotificationManagerCompat)
```

### Package structure (`packages/`)
| Package | Depends on | Key libs |
|---|---|---|
| `domain` | nothing | pure Dart |
| `data` | `domain` | drift, sqflite |
| `geofencing` | `domain` | flutter_background_geolocation OR platform channels |
| `notifications` | `domain` | flutter_local_notifications |
| `ui_kit` | nothing | Flutter widgets, theme |
| `apps/mobile` | all packages | Flutter app shell |

### Data model (SQLite, on-device)
```
todo_list        { id, title, color, notify_on_enter, notify_on_exit, geofence_id FK, sort_order, created_at }
todo_item        { id, list_id FK, title, notes, is_done, due_date, priority }
geofence_region  { id, latitude, longitude, radius_m, label, is_active, trigger, created_at }
```
Index: `todo_item(list_id, is_done)` for fast incomplete-item counts.

### Geofence event flow
```
User sets location on list
  → RegisterGeofenceUseCase → write to SQLite + register with OS API
  → [user moves] → OS fires region entry event → wakes app in background
  → GeofenceEventHandler → query SQLite for matched list → post local notification
  → Tap notification → deep-link to list by listId (passed in notification payload)
```

### iOS geofence limits + mitigation
- Hard OS limit: 20 simultaneous monitored regions
- Strategy: priority queue sorted by distance from current location; swap nearest 20 active regions as user moves using `CLLocationManager.distanceFilter`
- Background mode: `location` with `allowsBackgroundLocationUpdates = true`, `pausesLocationUpdatesAutomatically = true`
- Minimum radius: 100m

### Android geofence
- Limit: 100 simultaneous regions via `GeofencingClient`
- Events delivered via `PendingIntent` → `BroadcastReceiver` → `WorkManager`

---

## Use cases (domain layer)
| Use case | Description |
|---|---|
| `CreateListUseCase` | Create a named todo list with optional color |
| `DeleteListUseCase` | Delete list, items, and associated geofence |
| `AssignLocationUseCase` | Attach a lat/lng + radius to a list |
| `RegisterGeofenceUseCase` | Write geofence to DB + register with OS |
| `UnregisterGeofenceUseCase` | Remove from DB + deregister from OS |
| `ToggleItemUseCase` | Mark todo item done/undone |
| `ReorderListsUseCase` | Update sort_order for drag-and-drop |
| `HandleGeofenceEntryUseCase` | Query matched list, build + post notification |
| `RefreshActiveGeofencesUseCase` | Re-evaluate priority queue on location update |

---

## Screens
| Screen | Key behaviours |
|---|---|
| List dashboard | Shows all lists, incomplete item count badge, tap → detail |
| List detail | CRUD todo items, shows attached location chip |
| Map picker | Full-screen map, tap to place pin, drag slider for radius |
| Location detail | View/edit attached geofence, toggle notify on enter/exit |
| Notification settings | Per-list notification prefs, quiet hours |
| Permissions onboarding | Explains always-on location need before system prompt |

---

## CI / release pipeline

### Repo
- Single monorepo, tool: `melos`
- `melos.yaml` at root; workflows at `.github/workflows/`

### CI (`ci.yml`) — triggers: push + PR to `main`/`develop`
```
analyze (ubuntu)
  ├── test:domain (ubuntu, parallel)
  ├── test:data (ubuntu, parallel)
  └── test:geofencing (ubuntu, parallel)
        └── coverage (merge lcov → Codecov)
              ├── build:ios (macos-14, push only)
              └── build:android (ubuntu, push only)
```

### Release (`release.yml`) — trigger: `v*.*.*` tag push
```
release-ios   → sign IPA → TestFlight
release-android → sign AAB → Play Store internal track
github-release  → auto-generate release notes from tag
```

### Required GitHub secrets
`IOS_DISTRIBUTION_CERT_P12`, `IOS_DISTRIBUTION_CERT_PASSWORD`, `APPSTORE_ISSUER_ID`, `APPSTORE_API_KEY_ID`, `APPSTORE_API_PRIVATE_KEY`, `ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64`, `ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD`, `ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS`, `ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD`, `PLAY_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON`

---

## Build phases

### Phase 1 — Core CRUD
- Scaffold monorepo, melos, CI skeleton
- `domain` package: entities + use cases (no location yet)
- `data` package: drift schema, migrations, repositories
- `ui_kit`: design system, theme, base widgets
- List dashboard + list detail screens (no map)
- Full unit test coverage on `domain` and `data`

### Phase 2 — Location assignment
- Map picker screen (flutter_map or google_maps_flutter)
- `AssignLocationUseCase` + `RegisterGeofenceUseCase`
- Geofence written to DB, registered with OS
- Location permissions onboarding flow
- No background events yet — validate registration only

### Phase 3 — Background events + notifications
- `geofencing` package: platform bridge or flutter_background_geolocation
- `GeofenceEventHandler` wired to `HandleGeofenceEntryUseCase`
- `notifications` package: local notification posting + deep-link routing
- iOS priority queue + region swapping (`RefreshActiveGeofencesUseCase`)
- End-to-end test: enter region → notification fires → tap → correct list opens

### Phase 4 — Polish + release
- Notification grouping, snooze, quiet hours
- List sort/reorder drag-and-drop
- Empty states, permission denial handling, edge case UX
- Release workflow: TestFlight + Play Store internal
- Performance audit: geofence handler must complete within 10s iOS background budget

---

## Key constraints
- Zero network calls at runtime — all data on-device
- SQLite file in app private documents directory, excluded from iCloud backup (`isExcludedFromBackup = true`)
- Always-on location permission required; must be justified in `Info.plist` usage string and in onboarding UI
- Background handler budget: ≤10s on iOS
- Bundle ID: `com.yourcompany.localist` (update before Phase 4)
"}

{"description":"## Overview
Set up the Flutter monorepo structure using melos. Create all package skeletons so subsequent tasks have the correct directory layout and `pubspec.yaml` stubs to build against.

## What already exists (do NOT recreate)
- `melos.yaml` at repo root — already fully configured with analyze/format/test/build/gen scripts
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — complete CI pipeline already in place
- `.github/workflows/release.yml` — complete release pipeline already in place
- `.gitignore` at repo root

## What needs to be created
Directory/package structure:

```
apps/
  mobile/           ← Flutter app shell (iOS + Android targets)
packages/
  domain/           ← Pure Dart package, zero Flutter SDK dependency
  data/             ← Flutter plugin package (needs dart:io, sqflite FFI)
  geofencing/       ← Flutter plugin package (needs platform channels)
  notifications/    ← Flutter package (needs flutter_local_notifications)
  ui_kit/           ← Flutter package (widgets + theme)
```

## Steps

### 1. Create packages

```bash
# Pure Dart — domain must NOT depend on Flutter SDK
dart create --template=package packages/domain

# Flutter packages
flutter create --template=package packages/data
flutter create --template=package packages/geofencing
flutter create --template=package packages/notifications
flutter create --template=package packages/ui_kit

# App shell
flutter create --org com.yourcompany --project-name whileyoureout apps/mobile
```

### 2. Set pubspec.yaml for each package

**packages/domain/pubspec.yaml**
```yaml
name: domain
description: Pure-Dart domain layer — entities, use cases, repository interfaces.
publish_to: none
version: 0.1.0

environment:
  sdk: '>=3.2.0 <4.0.0'

dependencies:
  equatable: ^2.0.5
  uuid: ^4.0.0

dev_dependencies:
  very_good_analysis: ^5.1.0
  mocktail: ^1.0.0
  test: ^1.24.0
```

**packages/data/pubspec.yaml**
```yaml
name: data
description: SQLite data layer — drift schema and repository implementations.
publish_to: none
version: 0.1.0

environment:
  sdk: '>=3.2.0 <4.0.0'
  flutter: '>=3.16.0'

dependencies:
  flutter:
    sdk: flutter
  domain:
    path: ../domain
  drift: ^2.18.0
  sqlite3_flutter_libs: ^0.5.0
  path_provider: ^2.1.0
  path: ^1.9.0

dev_dependencies:
  very_good_analysis: ^5.1.0
  drift_dev: ^2.18.0
  build_runner: ^2.4.0
  flutter_test:
    sdk: flutter
```

**packages/geofencing/pubspec.yaml**
```yaml
name: geofencing
description: Geofence platform bridge — iOS CLLocationManager + Android GeofencingClient.
publish_to: none
version: 0.1.0

environment:
  sdk: '>=3.2.0 <4.0.0'
  flutter: '>=3.16.0'

dependencies:
  flutter:
    sdk: flutter
  domain:
    path: ../domain

dev_dependencies:
  very_good_analysis: ^5.1.0
  flutter_test:
    sdk: flutter
```

**packages/notifications/pubspec.yaml**
```yaml
name: notifications
description: Local notification service using flutter_local_notifications.
publish_to: none
version: 0.1.0

environment:
  sdk: '>=3.2.0 <4.0.0'
  flutter: '>=3.16.0'

dependencies:
  flutter:
    sdk: flutter
  domain:
    path: ../domain
  flutter_local_notifications: ^17.0.0

dev_dependencies:
  very_good_analysis: ^5.1.0
  flutter_test:
    sdk: flutter
```

**packages/ui_kit/pubspec.yaml**
```yaml
name: ui_kit
description: Shared design system — theme, colors, and base widgets.
publish_to: none
version: 0.1.0

environment:
  sdk: '>=3.2.0 <4.0.0'
  flutter: '>=3.16.0'

dependencies:
  flutter:
    sdk: flutter

dev_dependencies:
  very_good_analysis: ^5.1.0
  flutter_test:
    sdk: flutter
```

**apps/mobile/pubspec.yaml** (key additions — merge with generated file)
```yaml
dependencies:
  flutter:
    sdk: flutter
  domain:
    path: ../../packages/domain
  data:
    path: ../../packages/data
  geofencing:
    path: ../../packages/geofencing
  notifications:
    path: ../../packages/notifications
  ui_kit:
    path: ../../packages/ui_kit
  flutter_riverpod: ^2.5.0
  go_router: ^14.0.0
  shared_preferences: ^2.2.0
  permission_handler: ^11.0.0
  flutter_map: ^7.0.0
  latlong2: ^0.9.0
  geolocator: ^12.0.0

dev_dependencies:
  very_good_analysis: ^5.1.0
  flutter_test:
    sdk: flutter
  mocktail: ^1.0.0
```

### 3. Add analysis_options.yaml to every package
Each package needs `analysis_options.yaml` at its root:
```yaml
include: package:very_good_analysis/analysis_options.yaml
```

### 4. Add placeholder lib files
Each package needs a non-empty `lib/<name>.dart` barrel file so `melos analyze` does not error:
```dart
// lib/domain.dart
library domain;
```
(same pattern for data, geofencing, notifications, ui_kit)

### 5. Add placeholder tests
Each package needs `test/<name>_test.dart`:
```dart
void main() {}  // placeholder — real tests added in subsequent tasks
```

### 6. Verify workspace
```bash
melos bootstrap
melos analyze
melos format
melos test
```

All commands must pass before this task is considered complete.

## Key constraints
- `domain` package must use `dart create`, NOT `flutter create` — it must have zero Flutter SDK dependency
- Bundle ID for the app: `com.yourcompany.whileyoureout`
- iOS deployment target: 14.0 (set in `apps/mobile/ios/Podfile`: `platform :ios, '14.0'`)
- Android minSdk: 23 (set in `apps/mobile/android/app/build.gradle`)
- The `melos.yaml` scope filters rely on the package `name` field matching exactly: 'domain', 'data', 'geofencing'

## Acceptance criteria
- `melos bootstrap` succeeds with no errors
- `melos analyze` passes across all packages
- `melos format` reports no changes needed
- `melos test` runs placeholder tests for all packages with a `test/` dir
- Directory structure exactly matches the layout above"}

{"id":"while-youre-out-qys","title":"Phase 1: Scaffold monorepo, melos config, and CI skeleton","description":"## Overview
Set up the Flutter monorepo structure using melos, configure the workspace, and establish CI skeleton.

## What already exists
- `melos.yaml` is already present at the repo root with scripts for analyze, format, test, build, gen, coverage, clean, outdated, upgrade
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` already exists with full analyze/test/build pipeline
- `.github/workflows/release.yml` already exists with TestFlight + Play Store + GitHub release jobs
- `.gitignore` exists at repo root

## What needs to be created
Create the following directory/package structure (all packages are Flutter/Dart packages):

```
apps/
  mobile/           ← Flutter app shell (depends on all packages)
packages/
  domain/           ← Pure Dart package, no dependencies
  data/             ← Depends on domain; uses drift for SQLite
  geofencing/       ← Depends on domain; platform channels
  notifications/    ← Depends on domain; flutter_local_notifications
  ui_kit/           ← Flutter package; design system, theme, base widgets
```

## Steps
1. Run `flutter create` or `dart create` for each package with appropriate package names:
   - `apps/mobile`: `flutter create apps/mobile --org com.yourcompany --project-name whileyoureout`
   - `packages/domain`: `dart create packages/domain --template=package`
   - `packages/data`: `flutter create packages/data --template=package`
   - `packages/geofencing`: `flutter create packages/geofencing --template=package`
   - `packages/notifications`: `flutter create packages/notifications --template=package`
   - `packages/ui_kit`: `flutter create packages/ui_kit --template=package`

2. Set `pubspec.yaml` dependencies for each package:
   - `domain`: no dependencies (pure Dart). SDK: '>=3.2.0 <4.0.0'
   - `data`: depends on `domain`, adds drift: ^2.18.0, sqlite3_flutter_libs: ^0.5.0, path: ^1.9.0, path_provider: ^2.1.0
   - `geofencing`: depends on `domain` (path dependency). Add placeholder lib file.
   - `notifications`: depends on `domain`. Add flutter_local_notifications: ^17.0.0
   - `ui_kit`: no package deps beyond Flutter SDK
   - `apps/mobile`: path dependencies on all 5 packages

3. Verify `melos bootstrap` runs without errors.

4. Ensure each package has a `test/` directory with a placeholder test file so CI doesn't fail.

5. Verify `melos analyze` and `melos format` pass.

## Key constraints
- SDK: '>=3.2.0 <4.0.0', flutter: '>=3.16.0'
- Use `very_good_analysis: ^5.1.0` as a dev dependency in all packages for linting
- Bundle ID: `com.yourcompany.whileyoureout`
- The existing `melos.yaml` scripts must work (test:domain filters by scope 'domain', test:data by 'data', test:geofencing by 'geofencing')

## Acceptance criteria
- `melos bootstrap` succeeds
- `melos analyze` passes with no errors
- `melos format` passes
- `melos test` runs and all placeholder tests pass
- Directory structure matches the package layout above","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"branford.harris@bybeam.co","created_at":"2026-03-30T20:56:25Z","created_by":"Branford Harris","updated_at":"2026-03-30T20:56:25Z"}

{"id":"while-youre-out-ec0","title":"Phase 2: Map picker screen and location assignment","description":"## Overview
Implement the Map Picker screen that lets users tap to place a geofence pin on a map and set the radius. Wire up `AssignLocationUseCase` and `RegisterGeofenceUseCase` to write the geofence to SQLite and register it with the OS.

## Depends on
- while-youre-out-qb1 (dashboard + list detail screens must exist first)

## Package location
`apps/mobile/lib/screens/map_picker/` (screen)
`packages/geofencing/` (OS geofence registration — stub for Phase 2 validation only)

## Map library choice
Use `flutter_map: ^7.0.0` with OpenStreetMap tiles (no API key required, good for privacy-focused app).
```yaml
# apps/mobile pubspec.yaml additions
flutter_map: ^7.0.0
latlong2: ^0.9.0
```

## Screen: Map Picker (`lib/screens/map_picker/`)

### `MapPickerScreen`
- Full-screen map (FlutterMap widget)
- Default center: user's current location (request `location_when_in_use` permission here if not already granted, fall back to a default coordinate if denied)
- Tap on map: drops a pin at tapped coordinate, shows a circle overlay for the radius
- A bottom sheet (non-dismissible, partial height) shows:
  - Address label text field (auto-populated via reverse geocoding if possible, editable) — optional: can skip reverse geocoding and just show lat/lng as "Lat: x.xxx, Lng: y.yyy"
  - `RadiusSlider` (from ui_kit): range 100m–5000m, default 200m
  - Circle overlay on map updates live as slider moves
  - "Save Location" button (disabled until pin placed)
  - "Remove Location" button (visible only if list already has a geofence)
- On "Save Location" tap:
  1. Calls `AssignLocationUseCase` to upsert GeofenceRegion in DB and update list.geofenceId
  2. Calls `RegisterGeofenceUseCase` to register with OS (Phase 2: calls through to stub geofence service)
  3. Pops back to List Detail
- On "Remove Location" tap:
  1. Calls `UnregisterGeofenceUseCase`
  2. Clears list.geofenceId in DB
  3. Pops back

### `MapPickerViewModel`
- Exposes: selectedLatLng, radiusMeters, isSaving
- Methods: selectLocation(LatLng), updateRadius(double), saveLocation(), removeLocation()

## Geofencing package stub (Phase 2 scope)
In `packages/geofencing/lib/src/`, create `StubGeofenceService` that implements `GeofenceService` from domain:
- `registerRegion`: logs "[Stub] Registered geofence ${region.id}" — no actual OS call yet
- `unregisterRegion`: logs "[Stub] Unregistered ${regionId}"
- `events`: returns `Stream.empty()`

Wire `StubGeofenceService` into DI in `apps/mobile/lib/main.dart` for now.

## Location permissions onboarding flow

### `PermissionsOnboardingScreen` (`lib/screens/onboarding/`)
- Shown once at first launch (persisted via `shared_preferences: ^2.2.0`)
- Two-step: 
  1. "When in Use" — needed for map picker. Show rationale, then call `permission_handler` to request.
  2. "Always On" — needed for background geofence events (Phase 3). Show rationale explaining why background location is needed: "So we can remind you when you arrive at your saved locations". Then request.
- Use `permission_handler: ^11.0.0`
- If user denies "Always", show a non-blocking warning and allow continuing (the geofence won't fire in background, but the app is still usable)
- After onboarding, set `prefs.setBool('onboarding_complete', true)`

### Navigation integration
In `main.dart` router: if `onboarding_complete` is false → redirect to onboarding before dashboard.

## `Info.plist` keys required (iOS)
Add to `apps/mobile/ios/Runner/Info.plist`:
- `NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription`: "While You're Out uses your location to show it on the map when you set a reminder location."
- `NSLocationAlwaysAndWhenInUseUsageDescription`: "While You're Out needs background location access to notify you when you arrive at a saved location."
- `NSLocationAlwaysUsageDescription`: (same as above, for iOS <11 compatibility)

## Android permissions required
Add to `apps/mobile/android/app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml`:
```xml
<uses-permission android:name="android.permission.ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION" />
<uses-permission android:name="android.permission.ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATION" />
<uses-permission android:name="android.permission.ACCESS_BACKGROUND_LOCATION" />
```

## Acceptance criteria
- User can tap map, adjust radius, save — geofence is written to SQLite
- Location chip on List Detail now shows the label instead of "coming soon"
- `StubGeofenceService.registerRegion` is called and logs to console
- Onboarding flow runs on first launch, permission rationale is shown
- Tests pass: MapPickerViewModel unit tests (selectLocation, updateRadius, saveLocation calls use cases)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"branford.harris@bybeam.co","created_at":"2026-03-30T20:58:47Z","created_by":"Branford Harris","updated_at":"2026-03-30T20:58:47Z"}

{"id":"while-youre-out-7mq","title":"Phase 3: geofencing package — OS platform bridge (iOS + Android)","description":"## Overview
Replace the `StubGeofenceService` with a real platform implementation in `packages/geofencing`. On iOS, use `CLLocationManager` region monitoring. On Android, use `GeofencingClient`.

## Depends on
- while-youre-out-ec0 (Phase 2 must be complete; StubGeofenceService already wired in)

## Package location
`packages/geofencing/`

## Library choice
Use `flutter_background_geolocation` (transistorsoft, requires license for production but has a free dev license) OR implement via platform channels.

**Recommended: Use `geolocator: ^12.0.0` for location + implement geofence registration via platform channels** to avoid licensing issues. Alternatively, use `geofencing_api: ^1.0.0` (pure Dart wrapper around both platforms).

**Decision**: Use `geofencing_api` package if available and maintained, otherwise use platform channels. The agent should check pub.dev for the best available package that:
1. Supports iOS CLLocationManager region monitoring
2. Supports Android GeofencingClient
3. Does NOT require a paid license
4. Works with background execution

If using platform channels, create:
- `ios/Classes/GeofencingPlugin.swift` — wraps `CLLocationManager`
- `android/src/main/kotlin/.../GeofencingPlugin.kt` — wraps `GeofencingClient`

## `RealGeofenceService` (lib/src/real_geofence_service.dart)
Implements `GeofenceService` from domain:

```dart
class RealGeofenceService implements GeofenceService {
  @override
  Future<void> registerRegion(GeofenceRegion region) async {
    // Register with platform geofencing API
    // Region ID = region.id
    // Lat/Lng = region.latitude/longitude
    // Radius = max(region.radiusMeters, 100)  // enforce minimum 100m
    // Trigger based on region.trigger enum
  }

  @override
  Future<void> unregisterRegion(String regionId) async { ... }

  @override
  Future<void> unregisterAll() async { ... }

  @override
  Stream<GeofenceEvent> get events => _eventsController.stream;
}
```

## iOS-specific: Priority queue / region swapping
iOS allows max 20 monitored regions. Implement `RefreshActiveGeofencesUseCase` integration:

In `apps/mobile/lib/`, create `GeofenceManager` class that:
1. Subscribes to significant location changes (`distanceFilter: 500` meters)
2. On each location update, calls `RefreshActiveGeofencesUseCase` with current lat/lng
3. `RefreshActiveGeofencesUseCase` sorts all geofences by distance from current location, takes nearest 20, registers them, unregisters the rest

Enable in `Info.plist` (already added in Phase 2):
- `UIBackgroundModes`: add `location`
- `allowsBackgroundLocationUpdates = true` in `CLLocationManager`
- `pausesLocationUpdatesAutomatically = true`

## Android-specific
- Events delivered via `PendingIntent` → `BroadcastReceiver` → `WorkManager`
- Create `GeofenceBroadcastReceiver` in `android/src/main/kotlin/.../`
- Register in `AndroidManifest.xml`:
```xml
<receiver android:name=".GeofenceBroadcastReceiver" android:exported="true" />
```
- Add `work-manager` dependency in `android/app/build.gradle`: `implementation "androidx.work:work-runtime-ktx:2.9.0"`

## Wire up in DI
In `apps/mobile/lib/main.dart`, replace `StubGeofenceService` with `RealGeofenceService`.

## Tests (test/)
Use mock platform channel in Flutter tests:
- Mock platform channel to simulate geofence events
- Test that `events` stream emits `GeofenceEvent` when platform fires enter event
- Test `registerRegion` sends correct arguments over platform channel
- Test iOS priority queue: given 25 geofences, RefreshActiveGeofencesUseCase registers only 20 (nearest by haversine distance)

## Acceptance criteria
- `melos test:geofencing` passes
- On a physical device (or XCTest with location simulation), geofence entry event is received
- `melos analyze` passes","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"branford.harris@bybeam.co","created_at":"2026-03-30T20:59:11Z","created_by":"Branford Harris","updated_at":"2026-03-30T20:59:11Z"}

{"description":"## Overview
Polish pass for Phase 4: drag-and-drop list reordering, notification quiet hours, notification grouping on Android, Location Detail screen, permission-denial recovery, empty states audit, list title/color editing, and a performance audit of the geofence background handler.

## Depends on
- while-youre-out-v3r (end-to-end geofence → notification pipeline must be working)

## Feature 1: Drag-and-drop list reordering (apps/mobile/lib/screens/dashboard/)

Replace `ListView.builder` in `DashboardScreen` with `ReorderableListView.builder`:
```dart
ReorderableListView.builder(
  onReorder: (int oldIndex, int newIndex) {
    if (newIndex > oldIndex) newIndex--;  // adjust for removal
    viewModel.reorderLists(oldIndex, newIndex);
  },
  itemCount: lists.length,
  itemBuilder: (context, index) => AppListTile(
    key: ValueKey(lists[index].id),
    ...
    trailing: ReorderableDragStartListener(
      index: index,
      child: const Icon(Icons.drag_handle),
    ),
  ),
)
```

`DashboardViewModel.reorderLists(int oldIndex, int newIndex)`:
1. Reorder the local list copy
2. Extract ordered IDs
3. Call `ReorderListsUseCase(orderedIds)`
4. `notifyListeners()`

## Feature 2: Location Detail screen (apps/mobile/lib/screens/location_detail/)

This screen was specified in the epic but not yet implemented. Add it as a route reachable from `ListDetailScreen` → location chip tap (instead of going directly to the map picker).

Route: `/list/:listId/location`

`LocationDetailScreen` shows:
- Map thumbnail (non-interactive mini FlutterMap, centered on the geofence, with the radius circle overlay)
- Geofence label (editable inline text field)
- Radius value (read-only summary: "200 m")
- Two toggles: "Notify on arrival" (`notifyOnEnter`) and "Notify on departure" (`notifyOnExit`) — these update the `TodoList` record via `saveList`
- "Edit Location" button → navigates to `/list/:listId/map` (MapPickerScreen)
- "Remove Location" button → calls `UnregisterGeofenceUseCase` + clears `list.geofenceId`, pops back

Update `ListDetailScreen` location chip tap to navigate to `/list/:listId/location` instead of directly to the map picker.

## Feature 3: Notification grouping (Android — packages/notifications/)

In `FlutterNotificationService.postListNotification`:
```dart
const androidDetails = AndroidNotificationDetails(
  'geofence_alerts',
  'Location Reminders',
  channelDescription: 'Notifications when you arrive at a saved location',
  importance: Importance.high,
  priority: Priority.high,
  groupKey: 'com.yourcompany.whileyoureout.geofence_group',
);
```

After posting an individual notification, count active geofence notifications and post a summary if >1:
```dart
// Summary notification (Android only)
if (Platform.isAndroid) {
  await _plugin.show(
    0,  // fixed ID for the summary
    'While You\'re Out',
    'Multiple location reminders',
    const NotificationDetails(
      android: AndroidNotificationDetails(
        'geofence_alerts',
        'Location Reminders',
        groupKey: 'com.yourcompany.whileyoureout.geofence_group',
        setAsGroupSummary: true,
      ),
    ),
  );
}
```

## Feature 4: Quiet hours (apps/mobile/lib/screens/notification_settings/)

`NotificationSettingsScreen` at route `/settings/notifications`:
- `SwitchListTile` for "Enable quiet hours" — persisted via `shared_preferences` key `'quiet_hours_enabled'`
- Start time picker (default 22:00) — persisted as `'quiet_hours_start'` (int minutes since midnight, default 1320)
- End time picker (default 07:00) — persisted as `'quiet_hours_end'` (int minutes since midnight, default 420)
- Use Flutter's `showTimePicker` dialog

Tap pickers: show `showTimePicker()`, save selected time.

Access from Dashboard AppBar overflow menu (⋮):
```dart
PopupMenuButton(
  itemBuilder: (_) => [
    const PopupMenuItem(value: 'settings', child: Text('Notification settings')),
  ],
  onSelected: (value) {
    if (value == 'settings') context.push('/settings/notifications');
  },
)
```

In `FlutterNotificationService.postListNotification`:
```dart
final prefs = await SharedPreferences.getInstance();
final quietEnabled = prefs.getBool('quiet_hours_enabled') ?? false;
if (quietEnabled) {
  final now = TimeOfDay.now();
  final nowMinutes = now.hour * 60 + now.minute;
  final start = prefs.getInt('quiet_hours_start') ?? 1320;
  final end = prefs.getInt('quiet_hours_end') ?? 420;
  final inQuietHours = start > end
      ? (nowMinutes >= start || nowMinutes < end)  // overnight range
      : (nowMinutes >= start && nowMinutes < end);
  if (inQuietHours) return;  // suppress silently
}
```

## Feature 5: Permission denial handling (apps/mobile/lib/screens/)

In both `MapPickerScreen` and `PermissionsOnboardingScreen`:

**Permanently denied (locationWhenInUse):**
```dart
final status = await Permission.locationWhenInUse.status;
if (status.isPermanentlyDenied) {
  showDialog(context: context, builder: (_) => AlertDialog(
    title: const Text('Location permission required'),
    content: const Text('Please enable location access in your device Settings.'),
    actions: [
      TextButton(onPressed: () => Navigator.pop(context), child: const Text('Cancel')),
      TextButton(onPressed: () { openAppSettings(); Navigator.pop(context); },
                 child: const Text('Open Settings')),
    ],
  ));
  return;
}
```

**Always denied but When-In-Use granted:**
In `ListDetailScreen`, add a `MaterialBanner` (persistent, closeable with "Fix" action) when:
- `list.geofenceId != null`
- `Permission.locationAlways.status == PermissionStatus.denied || .limited`
```dart
MaterialBanner(
  content: const Text('Background location denied — arrival reminders won\'t work.'),
  actions: [
    TextButton(onPressed: openAppSettings, child: const Text('Fix')),
    TextButton(onPressed: () => ScaffoldMessenger.of(context).hideCurrentMaterialBanner(),
               child: const Text('Dismiss')),
  ],
)
```

## Feature 6: Empty states audit

Verify every screen has its `AppEmptyState` implemented:
| Screen | Icon | Headline | Body |
|---|---|---|---|
| Dashboard | `Icons.checklist` | "No lists yet" | "Tap + to create your first list" |
| List detail | `Icons.edit_note` | "Nothing here yet" | "Add your first item below" |
| Map picker (no pin placed) | `Icons.location_on_outlined` | "Tap the map" | "Drop a pin to set your reminder location" |

## Feature 7: List title and color editing

In `ListDetailScreen` AppBar, add an edit icon (`Icons.edit_outlined`):
- Opens `EditListBottomSheet` (modal bottom sheet):
  - `TextFormField` pre-filled with current title, max 50 chars, auto-focus
  - `ColorPickerRow` pre-selected to current color
  - "Save" button → `listRepo.saveList(list.copyWith(title: newTitle, color: newColor))`
  - "Cancel" button

## Feature 8: Performance audit

Wrap `HandleGeofenceEntryUseCase` call in `GeofenceEventHandler` with a stopwatch in debug mode:
```dart
assert(() {
  final sw = Stopwatch()..start();
  handleEntry(event.regionId).then((_) {
    debugPrint('[Geofence] HandleEntry completed in ${sw.elapsedMilliseconds}ms');
    assert(sw.elapsedMilliseconds < 8000,
      'HandleGeofenceEntryUseCase exceeded 8s iOS background budget!');
  });
  return true;
}());
```

## Tests
- `DashboardViewModel.reorderLists`: verify `ReorderListsUseCase` called with correct ordered ID list after a reorder
- `LocationDetailScreen`: verify toggles update `notifyOnEnter`/`notifyOnExit`; "Edit Location" navigates to map picker
- `FlutterNotificationService`: quiet hours in range → `show` NOT called; quiet hours overnight range; outside range → `show` IS called
- `PermissionDenial`: permanently denied → AlertDialog shown; always denied + geofence attached → MaterialBanner shown in ListDetail

## Acceptance criteria
- Drag-and-drop reorder persists across app restarts
- Location Detail screen shows geofence info, toggles work
- Quiet hours suppress notifications during the configured window (including overnight wraparound)
- Permission denial flows do not crash the app and provide a path to fix
- All empty states render correctly on fresh install
- List title + color editing works end-to-end
- `melos analyze` passes with zero warnings"}

{"id":"while-youre-out-946","title":"Phase 4: Polish — drag-and-drop reorder, quiet hours, notification grouping, empty states, and edge case UX","description":"## Overview
Phase 4 polish pass: drag-and-drop list reordering, notification quiet hours, notification grouping, permission-denial recovery flows, and all remaining edge-case UX.

## Depends on
- while-youre-out-v3r (end-to-end notifications must be working)

## Features

### 1. Drag-and-drop list reordering
In `DashboardScreen`, replace `ListView` with `ReorderableListView`:
```dart
ReorderableListView.builder(
  onReorder: (oldIndex, newIndex) => viewModel.reorderLists(oldIndex, newIndex),
  ...
)
```
- `DashboardViewModel.reorderLists` must translate old/new indices to ordered ID list, then call `ReorderListsUseCase`
- Drag handle visible on the right side of each `AppListTile` (use `ReorderableDragStartListener`)
- Persisted to SQLite via `ReorderListsUseCase` (updates `sort_order` column)

### 2. Notification grouping (Android)
In `FlutterNotificationService.postListNotification`, set the Android group key so multiple location notifications are grouped in the notification tray:
```dart
androidDetails: const AndroidNotificationDetails(
  'geofence_alerts',
  'Location Reminders',
  groupKey: 'com.yourcompany.whileyoureout.geofence_group',
  setAsGroupSummary: false,
)
```
Post a summary notification when >1 notification exists:
`FlutterLocalNotificationsPlugin.show` with `setAsGroupSummary: true`.

### 3. Quiet hours (per-app, not per-list for Phase 4)
Add `NotificationSettingsScreen` at route `/settings/notifications`:
- Toggle: "Enable quiet hours"
- Time range picker: start time (default 22:00) and end time (default 07:00)
- Persisted via `shared_preferences`
- In `FlutterNotificationService.postListNotification`: check current time against quiet hours before posting. If in quiet hours, skip the notification silently.
- Link to this screen from an overflow menu (⋮) on the Dashboard AppBar.

### 4. Permission denial handling
In `MapPickerScreen` and `PermissionsOnboardingScreen`:
- If location permission is permanently denied (`permission_handler` returns `PermissionStatus.permanentlyDenied`):
  - Show a dialog: "Location permission is required. Please enable it in Settings."
  - Provide an "Open Settings" button that calls `openAppSettings()` from `permission_handler`
- If "Always" permission is denied but "When in Use" is granted:
  - Allow map picker to work (location shown on map)
  - Show a persistent warning banner on List Detail for lists with geofences: "Background location denied — arrival reminders won't work. Tap to fix."
  - Tapping the banner calls `openAppSettings()`

### 5. Empty states audit
Ensure all empty states are implemented with `AppEmptyState` widget:
- Dashboard: "No lists yet. Tap + to create one." (checklist icon)
- List detail: "Add your first item below" (pencil icon)
- Map picker — no geofence yet: show centered prompt "Tap the map to set a location" (pin icon)

### 6. List color editing
In List Detail AppBar, add an edit button (pencil icon) that opens `EditListBottomSheet`:
- Title text field (pre-filled)
- `ColorPickerRow` (pre-selected to current color)
- Save button calls `saveList` with updated title/color

### 7. Performance audit
Ensure `HandleGeofenceEntryUseCase` execution time is logged in debug mode:
```dart
final stopwatch = Stopwatch()..start();
await handleGeofenceEntry(regionId);
debugPrint('[Geofence] HandleEntry took ${stopwatch.elapsedMilliseconds}ms');
```
Assert (in debug only, not production): elapsedMs < 8000 (leaving 2s margin from 10s iOS budget).

## Tests
- DashboardViewModel.reorderLists: verify sortOrder updated correctly
- NotificationSettingsScreen: toggle quiet hours, verify persisted
- FlutterNotificationService: verify notification skipped during quiet hours
- PermissionDenial: widget test — permanently denied state shows dialog with "Open Settings" button

## Acceptance criteria
- Drag-and-drop reorder persists across app restarts
- Quiet hours suppress notifications correctly
- Permission denial flows don't crash the app and guide users to fix
- All empty states visible on fresh install
- List color edit works end-to-end
- `melos analyze` passes","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"branford.harris@bybeam.co","created_at":"2026-03-30T21:00:25Z","created_by":"Branford Harris","updated_at":"2026-03-30T21:00:25Z"}

{"id":"while-youre-out-qys","title":"Phase 1: Scaffold monorepo, melos config, and CI skeleton","description":"## Overview
Set up the Flutter monorepo structure using melos. Create all package skeletons so subsequent tasks have the correct directory layout and `pubspec.yaml` stubs to build against.

## What already exists (do NOT recreate)
- `melos.yaml` at repo root — already fully configured with analyze/format/test/build/gen scripts
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — complete CI pipeline already in place
- `.github/workflows/release.yml` — complete release pipeline already in place
- `.gitignore` at repo root

## What needs to be created
Directory/package structure:

```
apps/
  mobile/           ← Flutter app shell (iOS + Android targets)
packages/
  domain/           ← Pure Dart package, zero Flutter SDK dependency
  data/             ← Flutter plugin package (needs dart:io, sqflite FFI)
  geofencing/       ← Flutter plugin package (needs platform channels)
  notifications/    ← Flutter package (needs flutter_local_notifications)
  ui_kit/           ← Flutter package (widgets + theme)
```

## Steps

### 1. Create packages

```bash
# Pure Dart — domain must NOT depend on Flutter SDK
dart create --template=package packages/domain

# Flutter packages
flutter create --template=package packages/data
flutter create --template=package packages/geofencing
flutter create --template=package packages/notifications
flutter create --template=package packages/ui_kit

# App shell
flutter create --org com.yourcompany --project-name whileyoureout apps/mobile
```

### 2. Set pubspec.yaml for each package

**packages/domain/pubspec.yaml**
```yaml
name: domain
description: Pure-Dart domain layer — entities, use cases, repository interfaces.
publish_to: none
version: 0.1.0

environment:
  sdk: '>=3.2.0 <4.0.0'

dependencies:
  equatable: ^2.0.5
  uuid: ^4.0.0

dev_dependencies:
  very_good_analysis: ^5.1.0
  mocktail: ^1.0.0
  test: ^1.24.0
```

**packages/data/pubspec.yaml**
```yaml
name: data
description: SQLite data layer — drift schema and repository implementations.
publish_to: none
version: 0.1.0

environment:
  sdk: '>=3.2.0 <4.0.0'
  flutter: '>=3.16.0'

dependencies:
  flutter:
    sdk: flutter
  domain:
    path: ../domain
  drift: ^2.18.0
  sqlite3_flutter_libs: ^0.5.0
  path_provider: ^2.1.0
  path: ^1.9.0

dev_dependencies:
  very_good_analysis: ^5.1.0
  drift_dev: ^2.18.0
  build_runner: ^2.4.0
  flutter_test:
    sdk: flutter
```

**packages/geofencing/pubspec.yaml**
```yaml
name: geofencing
description: Geofence platform bridge — iOS CLLocationManager + Android GeofencingClient.
publish_to: none
version: 0.1.0

environment:
  sdk: '>=3.2.0 <4.0.0'
  flutter: '>=3.16.0'

dependencies:
  flutter:
    sdk: flutter
  domain:
    path: ../domain

dev_dependencies:
  very_good_analysis: ^5.1.0
  flutter_test:
    sdk: flutter
```

**packages/notifications/pubspec.yaml**
```yaml
name: notifications
description: Local notification service using flutter_local_notifications.
publish_to: none
version: 0.1.0

environment:
  sdk: '>=3.2.0 <4.0.0'
  flutter: '>=3.16.0'

dependencies:
  flutter:
    sdk: flutter
  domain:
    path: ../domain
  flutter_local_notifications: ^17.0.0

dev_dependencies:
  very_good_analysis: ^5.1.0
  flutter_test:
    sdk: flutter
```

**packages/ui_kit/pubspec.yaml**
```yaml
name: ui_kit
description: Shared design system — theme, colors, and base widgets.
publish_to: none
version: 0.1.0

environment:
  sdk: '>=3.2.0 <4.0.0'
  flutter: '>=3.16.0'

dependencies:
  flutter:
    sdk: flutter

dev_dependencies:
  very_good_analysis: ^5.1.0
  flutter_test:
    sdk: flutter
```

**apps/mobile/pubspec.yaml** (key additions — merge with generated file)
```yaml
dependencies:
  flutter:
    sdk: flutter
  domain:
    path: ../../packages/domain
  data:
    path: ../../packages/data
  geofencing:
    path: ../../packages/geofencing
  notifications:
    path: ../../packages/notifications
  ui_kit:
    path: ../../packages/ui_kit
  flutter_riverpod: ^2.5.0
  go_router: ^14.0.0
  shared_preferences: ^2.2.0
  permission_handler: ^11.0.0
  flutter_map: ^7.0.0
  latlong2: ^0.9.0
  geolocator: ^12.0.0

dev_dependencies:
  very_good_analysis: ^5.1.0
  flutter_test:
    sdk: flutter
  mocktail: ^1.0.0
```

### 3. Add analysis_options.yaml to every package
Each package needs `analysis_options.yaml` at its root:
```yaml
include: package:very_good_analysis/analysis_options.yaml
```

### 4. Add placeholder lib files
Each package needs a non-empty `lib/<name>.dart` barrel file so `melos analyze` does not error:
```dart
// lib/domain.dart
library domain;
```
(same pattern for data, geofencing, notifications, ui_kit)

### 5. Add placeholder tests
Each package needs `test/<name>_test.dart`:
```dart
void main() {}  // placeholder — real tests added in subsequent tasks
```

### 6. Verify workspace
```bash
melos bootstrap
melos analyze
melos format
melos test
```

All commands must pass before this task is considered complete.

## Key constraints
- `domain` package must use `dart create`, NOT `flutter create` — it must have zero Flutter SDK dependency
- Bundle ID for the app: `com.yourcompany.whileyoureout`
- iOS deployment target: 14.0 (set in `apps/mobile/ios/Podfile`: `platform :ios, '14.0'`)
- Android minSdk: 23 (set in `apps/mobile/android/app/build.gradle`)
- The `melos.yaml` scope filters rely on the package `name` field matching exactly: 'domain', 'data', 'geofencing'

## Acceptance criteria
- `melos bootstrap` succeeds with no errors
- `melos analyze` passes across all packages
- `melos format` reports no changes needed
- `melos test` runs placeholder tests for all packages with a `test/` dir
- Directory structure exactly matches the layout above","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"branford.harris@bybeam.co","created_at":"2026-03-30T20:56:25Z","created_by":"Branford Harris","updated_at":"2026-03-30T21:15:19Z"}

new_value: Scaffolded full monorepo: all packages created, pubspecs set, analysis_options using very_good_analysis, placeholder libs and passing tests in every package, iOS 14.0 + Android minSdk 23 configured. melos bootstrap/analyze/format/test all pass.

old_value:
{"id":"while-youre-out-lk6","title":"Fix melos 'test' script — non-interactive execution fails with interactive prompt","description":"The melos.yaml 'test' script (melos run test) triggers an interactive package-selection prompt in non-TTY environments (CI, agent shells), causing it to crash with:

  StdinException: Error getting terminal echo mode

## Root cause
The melos exec --fail-fast in the test script is valid, but melos run test itself prompts the user to select a package when run without a filter.

## Fix
Update the 'test' script in melos.yaml to add packageFilters so it runs across all packages non-interactively, similar to how the analyze/format scripts work. For example:

  test:
    run: melos exec --fail-fast -- flutter test --coverage
    description: Run all unit and widget tests across all packages.
    packageFilters:
      dirExists: test

Verify by running: melos run test (should execute without prompting).

Note: domain uses 'dart test' not 'flutter test' — verify the flutter test command works for pure-Dart packages too (it does via the Flutter SDK's test runner).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"branford.harris@bybeam.co","created_at":"2026-03-30T21:52:47Z","created_by":"Branford Harris","updated_at":"2026-03-30T21:52:47Z"}

{"id":"while-youre-out-cg0","title":"Add .idea/ and *.iml to .gitignore to avoid committing IDE files","description":"During the monorepo scaffold (while-youre-out-qys), .idea/ run configurations and *.iml files were committed because they are not excluded in .gitignore.

## What to do
Add the following to the root .gitignore:
- .idea/
- *.iml
- melos_*.iml

Also consider adding pubspec_overrides.yaml to .gitignore since those are melos-generated local overrides and should not be committed.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"chore","owner":"branford.harris@bybeam.co","created_at":"2026-03-30T21:52:36Z","created_by":"Branford Harris","updated_at":"2026-03-30T21:52:36Z"}

new_value: Fixed: rewrote all melos scripts to use exec: key (melos v6 idiomatic) instead of 'run: melos exec ...' to avoid dependency on melos being in PATH. Added failFast: true and packageFilters: dirExists: test for the test script. Added .envrc to prepend ~/.pub-cache/bin to PATH. Verified melos run test --no-select works non-interactively.
Fixed: added .idea/, *.iml, melos_*.iml, and pubspec_overrides.yaml to .gitignore. Removed all previously-tracked IDE and melos-generated files from git index with git rm --cached.
Implemented full domain package: 4 entities, 3 repo interfaces, 2 service interfaces, 11 use cases, 34 passing tests, dart analyze: 0 issues
Duplicate of while-youre-out-05d — same title and description. Closing the less-detailed copy.

{"description":"Discovered while implementing while-youre-out-3r5 (domain package).

The melos.yaml test:domain script runs 'flutter test --coverage' but domain is a pure Dart package with no Flutter SDK dependency. 'flutter test' fails on pure Dart packages. Fix: change test:domain (and test:data if also pure Dart) to use 'dart test --coverage' instead.

Additionally, the coverage:combine script relies on 'dart pub global run coverage:format_coverage' which is not activated globally in the environment, so lcov.info is never populated after dart test runs. Either activate the coverage package in CI (dart pub global activate coverage) or adopt an alternative lcov generation approach.

The epic this is part of: while-youre-out-1pq."}

Fix: change test:domain (and test:data if also pure Dart) to use dart test --coverage. Additionally, the coverage:combine script relies on dart pub global run coverage:format_coverage which is not activated in CI, so lcov.info is never populated. Either activate the coverage package in CI or use an alternative approach.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"chore","owner":"branford.harris@bybeam.co","created_at":"2026-03-31T17:20:57Z","created_by":"Branford Harris","updated_at":"2026-03-31T17:20:57Z"}

{"id":"while-youre-out-aci","title":"Phase 1: ui_kit package — design system, theme, and base widgets","description":"## Overview
Implement the `packages/ui_kit` package: the app's design system, theme, reusable base widgets, and color constants.

## Depends on
- while-youre-out-qys (monorepo scaffold)

## Package location
`packages/ui_kit/`

## Key dependencies (pubspec.yaml)
```yaml
dependencies:
  flutter:
    sdk: flutter
dev_dependencies:
  very_good_analysis: ^5.1.0
  flutter_test:
    sdk: flutter
```

## Theme (lib/src/theme/)

### `AppTheme`
- Provide static getters: `AppTheme.light` → `ThemeData`, `AppTheme.dark` → `ThemeData`
- Use Material 3 (`useMaterial3: true`)
- Color seed: the same blue used as default list color (`0xFF2196F3`)
- Define `AppColors` constants class with the palette used across the app:
  - `AppColors.listColors` — list of 8 ARGB int values users can pick for list color
  - `AppColors.danger` — red for delete actions

## Typography (lib/src/theme/text_styles.dart)
Define `AppTextStyles` with named text style constants:
- `AppTextStyles.listTitle` — 18sp, medium weight
- `AppTextStyles.itemTitle` — 16sp, regular
- `AppTextStyles.badge` — 12sp, bold, for incomplete count badge
- `AppTextStyles.caption` — 12sp, muted, for dates/secondary info

## Base widgets (lib/src/widgets/)

### `AppScaffold`
Wraps `Scaffold` with consistent AppBar styling. Props: title, body, actions, floatingActionButton.

### `AppListTile`
Styled list tile with:
- Leading color dot (circle, 12px diameter, filled with list color)
- Title text
- Trailing badge showing incomplete item count (hidden if 0)
- Location pin icon if list has geofence attached

### `AppCheckbox`
Custom checkbox widget with strikethrough animation on todo items.

### `AppEmptyState`
Centered illustration + headline + body copy. Props: icon (IconData), headline (String), body (String), action (optional Widget).

### `AppBottomSheet`
Convenience wrapper for `showModalBottomSheet` with consistent padding and drag handle.

### `ColorPickerRow`
Horizontal scrollable row of 8 color circles. Tapping one selects it (checkmark overlay). Emits `onColorChanged(int color)`.

### `RadiusSlider`
A labeled slider for geofence radius. Range: 100m – 5000m. Shows formatted label: '250 m', '1.2 km'. Emits `onRadiusChanged(double meters)`.

## Barrel export
`lib/ui_kit.dart` — export AppTheme, AppColors, AppTextStyles, and all widgets.

## Tests
Widget tests for `AppCheckbox` (tap toggles), `ColorPickerRow` (tap fires callback), `RadiusSlider` (emits value in range), `AppListTile` (badge hidden when count = 0, shown when > 0).

## Acceptance criteria
- `melos analyze` passes
- All widgets have dartdoc comments
- Widget tests pass","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"branford.harris@bybeam.co","created_at":"2026-03-30T20:57:50Z","created_by":"Branford Harris","updated_at":"2026-03-30T20:57:50Z"}

new_value: Implemented full ui_kit package: AppColors, AppTextStyles, AppTheme (light+dark, Material 3), AppScaffold, AppListTile, AppCheckbox (animated strikethrough), AppEmptyState, AppBottomSheet, ColorPickerRow, RadiusSlider. 20 widget tests passing, dart analyze: 0 issues.
